acceptance.assertion: PyYAML installation for the Kubernetes security and validation workflows is bound through a hash-verified dependency mechanism rather than version-only pip resolution, without adding a second dependency authority.
All relevant Kubernetes workflow jobs that install the same validator dependency use the canonical pinned mechanism so the Trivy job does not create a weaker parallel supply-chain path.

Workflow permissions, immutable pull-request-head checkout binding, platform validation, Trivy scan behavior and required CI remain unchanged or stricter; exact diff review and required CI pass before merge.

approval.note: Registered from the T045 PR #1552 security review. PyYAML is version-pinned but not artifact-hash-pinned in the Kubernetes workflow. This is not a T045 merge blocker, but it is useful supply-chain hardening and must not remain only as prose.

goal: Replace version-only PyYAML bootstrap in Weltgewebe Kubernetes security and validation CI with one canonical hash-verified dependency path while preserving the existing platform and Trivy contracts.

does_not_establish: that the current PyYAML release is malicious or compromised
permission to replace the repository dependency authority
metadata.registered_from: OPERATOR-ECOSYSTEM-REDUNDANCY-V1-T045 / Weltgewebe PR #1552 security review on 2026-07-23

rollback.strategy: Revert only the dependency-bootstrap hardening. The already merged Trivy production path and platform validation contracts remain independently reversible.

title: Hash-pin Kubernetes Python validator bootstrap
